content_en:
One of the Ansaar said: "lam the rubbing post and the fruitful propped-up palm*. Let us have two rulers: one from us and another from you, O people of Quraysh." Altercation waxed hotter and voices were raised until I was afraid lest a complete disunity should take place, so I said, ‘Stretch out your hand, O Abu Bakr. He did so and I paid him homage, then the Muhajireen swore the pledge of allegiance and so did the Ansaar afterwards.

Le. aman who can cure people's ills and ts held in high esteem on account of his

great experience and good counsel, {trans.)

“Umar added: “By Allah, there was no grater problem than the election of Abu Bakr; we were afraid that if we left the people, they might pledge allegiance after us to one of their men, in which case we would have given them our consent for something against our real wish, or would have opposed them and stirred commotion. So if anyone pledge allegiance to someone without consulting other Muslims, then the one he has selected should not be given allegiance, lest both of them should be killed."

On the authority of Ibn Mas* ood, may Allah be pleased with him, who says: Upon the demise of Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, the Ansaar suggested that there should be two leaders (one from the Ansaar and another from the Muhajireen), until finally ~Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab intervened, saying: "O people of the Ansaar, know you not that Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, ordered Abu Bakr to lead the people in Salaal? Which of you will willingly take precedence over Abu Bakr?" Thereupon the Ansaar said, "Allah forbid that we should take precedence over Abu Bakr!"

fast to it, Allah will guide you as He guided him. Allah has placed your affairs in the hands of the best of you, the Companion of Allah's Messenger, and the "second of the two who were in the cave". So arise and pledge allegiance to him. Thereupon the people pledged allegiance to Abu Bakr. after the pledge in the hall (of Banu Saa* idah).

Then Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, got up, praised and thanked Allah as He deserves, and then addressed the assembly thus: "O people, I have been given authority over you, yet [ am not the best of you. if 1 do well, give me your support, and if I do wrong, set me right. Truth is loyalty, and lying is treachery. The weak amongst you shall be strong in my eyes, until I secure his rights, Jnsha-Allah (Allah willing); and the strong amongst you shall be weak in my eyes, until I wrest from him the rights of others, Insha-Allah. If a people give up Jihad in the Cause of Allah, Allah will smite them with humiliation. And if obscenity permeates among a people, Allah will inflict calamities upon them. Obey me as long as I obey Allah and His Messenger, and if [I disobey Allah and His Messenger, you owe me no obedience. Arise for Salaah, Allah have mercy upon you!"

Such was the Magna Charta the first Caliph of Islam, granted his people on the first day of his assuming post, without request.

Abu Bakr Sends out Usamah's Punitive Expedition:

Some weeks before his death, the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, nominated Usamah Ibn Zayd, aged seventeen years then, to lead an expedition against the Levant.

He was to avenge the death of his father, Zayd, the freed slave of the Prophet , peace and blessings be upon him. Zayd was killed by the Levantines in the battle of Mu‘tah. The preparations of the expedition were under way when the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, fell seriously ill and later passed away. That held up Usamah's expedition for some weeks.

As soon as Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, assumed the Caliphate, the first thing he thought of was the dispatch of Usamah's punitive expedition. The death of the Prophet led some people to think that the light of [slam was fading away, and that many tribes that had entered the fold of Islam only a short time before were by no means firm in faith and many of them had started to show signs of bolting out of the fold of Islam. All this showed that things were not going to be easy at all for the incumbent Caliph, Abu Bakr.

However, Abu Bakr had to carry out the commands of the Prophet at all costs. He was determined to send out the expedition initiated by the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him. Some of the Companions suggested to Abu Bakr to better drop the idea for the time being claiming that the time was not ripe for that as trouble was brewing all around, and as such it was unwise to send troops out when they were urgently needed to quell the situation at home. However, Abu Bakr would not listen to all this, rather, he said: "How can I fold up the flag which the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, himself unfurled?"

Then someone suggested that Usamah was too raw to lead the expedition, and that it was wiser to put a more experienced man in command. The suggestion caused Abu Bakr to get furious and said: "How can | dismiss a man whom the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him,

So the troops marched out under the command of Usamah, about three weeks after the passing away of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him. Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, accompanied Usamah some distance out of Madeenah. The youthful commander was riding a horse, while the Caliph walked by his side to boost his morale and give his soldiers more confidence in him. Usamah said, "O successor of the Prophet, what about you getting on the horse while [ get down to walk beside you?" "By Allah," replied Abu Bakr, "I will agree to neither of the two things. What harm is there if a little dust covers my feet, while I go some steps in the Cause of Allah? For every step one takes in Allah's Cause one gets the reward of seven hundred good deeds."

When Abu Bakr needed ~Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, may Allah be pleased with him, who was then one of the men under Usamah's command, to stay behind at Madeenah for purpose of consultation, he made a request to Usamah to allow * Umar stay behind, and Usamah agreed to his request.

Before bidding Muslim army farewell, Caliph Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, gave them useful advice and final instruction which serves as a code of conduct in war and this remains unsurpassed to this day, as they are still taught to school children throughout the Muslim World. Among those instructions were: "Never be dishonest nor treacherous. Do not steal from the booty you lay your hands on. Do not mutilate the dead bodies of the enemy. Do not kill the aged, children and women. Do not burn date-palms. Do not cut down fruit trees. Do not slaughter goats, or cows, or camel, except for nutritional purposes. You will come across people who have taken to asceticism and sitting in monasteries, so leave them alone."

Usamah's expedition proved very successful, as he raided the frontier districts of the Levant and was back in Madeenah after forty days.

The expedition had another good result. It proved an eyeopener to those who thought that Islam was dying out. They had a clear proof that Islam was still able to challenge one of the greatest powers of the world. This overawed the wavering tribes, and some of the tribes which had left Islam actually reentered its fold.

The consecutive victories of Muslims during the lifetime of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, had the great impact of bringing many tribes that lived faraway from Madeenah to the fold of Islam. Some of these tribes embraced Islam in admiration of the victory achieved by Muslims, some embraced it in order to gain some worldly benefit, and some accepted Islam out of deep faith and profound conviction. This means that a great number of Muslims had not yet got rid of the remnants of Jafiliyyah, particularly the manners and behaviors which characterized the people before Islam. Some Muslims were still plagued with racism and pride, that give rise to hatred and ill-feelings among tribes that brought them back to their pre-Islamic manners. Thus, the Islamic creed was not yet wellestablished in the hearts of some Muslims. Allah Almighty says:

"The desert Arabs say, "We believe." Say, "Ye have no Faith; but ye (only) say, 'We have submitted our wills to Allah,’ for not yet has Faith entered your hearts. But if ye obey Allah and His Messenger, He_ will not belittle aught oft your deeds: for Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful."" (Al-Hujoraat: 14)

That is why when ~Uyaynah Ibn Hisn, the chief of the tribe of Bani Fazarah, said when he supported Tulayhah, who claimed Prophet hood: "A Prophet from the two allies (i.e. the tribes of Asad and Ghatafaan) is far more better to us than a Prophet from Quraysh."

The spirit of racism of Jahilliyah reached its climax in some people who went to extreme and claimed to be Prophet s, in order to gain some pride and rank among their people not less than what Quraysh enjoyed when Islam prevailed in the lifetime of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him. This is attributed to their misunderstanding of Islam, the religion which does not distinguishes between Quraysh and other tribes, even if they are non-Arabs.

Thus, Al-Aswad Al-* Ansi, began to convince the tribe of Mudhajaj that he was a Prophet who received revelation from Heaven, and was supported by some of his people; Musaylamah Al-Khadhaab (the liar) claimed Prophet hood in the tribe of Banu Haeefah; and Tulayhah Al-Asadyy in the tribe of Banu Asad also claimed to be a Prophet; likewise, Sajaah claimed Prophet hood in the tribe of Banu Tameem.
content_id:
Salah seorang Anshar berkata: "Akulah tiang penopang dan pohon kurma yang berbuah lebat*. Biarlah kita memiliki dua pemimpin: satu dari kami dan satu lagi dari kalian, wahai kaum Quraysy." Perdebatan semakin memanas dan suara-suara meninggi hingga aku khawatir akan terjadi perpecahan total, maka aku berkata, 'Ulurkan tanganmu, wahai Abu Bakr.' Beliau melakukannya dan aku membai'atnya, kemudian kaum Muhajirin bersumpah setia dan kaum Anshar pun mengikutinya setelah itu.

Yakni seseorang yang mampu menyembuhkan penyakit manusia dan dihormati karena

pengalamannya yang luas dan nasihatnya yang baik. (penj.)

"Umar menambahkan: "Demi Allah, tidak ada masalah yang lebih besar daripada pemilihan Abu Bakr; kami khawatir jika kami meninggalkan orang-orang, mereka mungkin akan membai'at salah seorang dari mereka setelah kami pergi, yang dalam hal itu kami akan memberikan persetujuan terhadap sesuatu yang bertentangan dengan keinginan kami yang sebenarnya, atau kami akan menentang mereka dan menimbulkan kekacauan. Maka jika seseorang membai'at orang lain tanpa bermusyawarah dengan kaum Muslimin, maka orang yang dipilihnya tidak boleh dibai'at, agar keduanya tidak terbunuh."

Dari Ibn Mas'ud, semoga Allah meridhainya, yang berkata: Setelah wafatnya Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, kaum Anshar mengusulkan agar ada dua pemimpin (satu dari Anshar dan satu dari Muhajirin), hingga akhirnya 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab turun tangan, seraya berkata: "Wahai kaum Anshar, tidakkah kalian tahu bahwa Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, memerintahkan Abu Bakr untuk memimpin manusia dalam salah? Siapakah di antara kalian yang rela mendahului Abu Bakr?" Maka kaum Anshar berkata, "Kami berlindung kepada Allah dari mendahului Abu Bakr!"

berpeganglah kepadanya, niscaya Allah akan membimbing kalian sebagaimana Dia membimbingnya. Allah telah menyerahkan urusan kalian kepada orang terbaik di antara kalian, Sahabat Rasulullah, dan "orang kedua dari dua orang yang berada di dalam gua." Maka bangunlah dan bai'atlah dia. Maka orang-orang pun membai'at Abu Bakr, setelah bai'at di balai (Banu Sa'idah).

Kemudian Abu Bakr, semoga Allah meridhainya, berdiri, memuji dan bersyukur kepada Allah sebagaimana yang layak bagi-Nya, lalu berpidato di hadapan mereka: "Wahai manusia, aku telah diberi kekuasaan atas kalian, namun aku bukanlah yang terbaik di antara kalian. Jika aku berbuat baik, dukunglah aku, dan jika aku berbuat salah, luruskanlah aku. Kebenaran adalah kesetiaan, dan kebohongan adalah pengkhianatan. Orang yang lemah di antara kalian akan kuat di mataku, hingga aku menunaikan haknya, insya Allah; dan orang yang kuat di antara kalian akan lemah di mataku, hingga aku mengambil darinya hak orang lain, insya Allah. Jika suatu kaum meninggalkan jihad di jalan Allah, Allah akan menimpakan kehinaan kepada mereka. Dan jika kekejian merajalela di tengah suatu kaum, Allah akan menimpakan bencana kepada mereka. Taatlah kepadaku selama aku taat kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya, dan jika aku durhaka kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya, kalian tidak wajib taat kepadaku. Berdirilah untuk salah, semoga Allah merahmati kalian!"

Demikianlah piagam agung yang diberikan Khalifah pertama Islam kepada rakyatnya pada hari pertama ia menjabat, tanpa diminta.

Abu Bakr Memberangkatkan Ekspedisi Hukuman Usamah:

Beberapa pekan sebelum wafatnya, Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, menunjuk Usamah Ibn Zayd, yang saat itu berusia tujuh belas tahun, untuk memimpin ekspedisi ke Syam.

Ia ditugaskan untuk membalaskan kematian ayahnya, Zayd, budak yang dimerdekakan oleh Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam. Zayd terbunuh oleh pasukan Syam dalam Perang Mu'tah. Persiapan ekspedisi sedang berlangsung ketika Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, jatuh sakit parah dan kemudian wafat. Hal itu menunda ekspedisi Usamah selama beberapa pekan.

Segera setelah Abu Bakr, semoga Allah meridhainya, menjabat sebagai Khalifah, hal pertama yang ia pikirkan adalah pemberangkatan ekspedisi hukuman Usamah. Wafatnya Nabi membuat sebagian orang mengira bahwa cahaya Islam mulai meredup, dan bahwa banyak suku yang baru memeluk Islam belum kokoh dalam keimanan, serta banyak di antara mereka yang mulai menunjukkan tanda-tanda hendak keluar dari Islam. Semua ini menunjukkan bahwa keadaan tidak akan mudah sama sekali bagi Khalifah yang baru menjabat, Abu Bakr.

Meskipun demikian, Abu Bakr harus melaksanakan perintah Nabi dengan segala cara. Beliau bertekad untuk memberangkatkan ekspedisi yang diprakarsai oleh Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam. Sebagian Sahabat menyarankan Abu Bakr agar membatalkan rencana itu untuk sementara waktu, dengan alasan bahwa waktunya belum tepat karena masalah sedang bergejolak di mana-mana, dan tidaklah bijaksana mengirim pasukan keluar sementara mereka sangat dibutuhkan untuk mengatasi situasi di dalam negeri. Namun Abu Bakr tidak mau mendengarkan semua itu, bahkan beliau berkata: "Bagaimana mungkin aku melipat bendera yang Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, sendiri telah membentangkannya?"

Kemudian seseorang mengusulkan bahwa Usamah terlalu muda untuk memimpin ekspedisi, dan lebih bijaksana jika menempatkan orang yang lebih berpengalaman sebagai komandan. Usulan itu membuat Abu Bakr murka dan berkata: "Bagaimana mungkin aku memberhentikan seorang yang Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam,

Maka pasukan pun bergerak di bawah komando Usamah, sekitar tiga pekan setelah wafatnya Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam. Abu Bakr, semoga Allah meridhainya, menemani Usamah beberapa jarak dari Madinah. Komandan muda itu menunggang kuda, sementara Khalifah berjalan di sampingnya untuk menguatkan semangatnya dan memberikan kepercayaan lebih kepada para prajuritnya. Usamah berkata, "Wahai penerus Nabi, bagaimana jika engkau yang menunggang kuda sementara aku turun berjalan di sampingmu?" "Demi Allah," jawab Abu Bakr, "aku tidak akan menyetujui keduanya. Apa ruginya jika sedikit debu menutupi kakiku, sementara aku melangkah beberapa langkah di jalan Allah? Karena setiap langkah yang diambil seseorang di jalan Allah, ia mendapat pahala tujuh ratus kebaikan."

Ketika Abu Bakr membutuhkan 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab, semoga Allah meridhainya, yang saat itu termasuk di antara pasukan di bawah komando Usamah, untuk tetap tinggal di Madinah guna keperluan musyawarah, beliau mengajukan permintaan kepada Usamah agar mengizinkan 'Umar tetap tinggal, dan Usamah menyetujui permintaannya.

Sebelum melepas pasukan Muslim, Khalifah Abu Bakr, semoga Allah meridhainya, memberikan nasihat berguna dan instruksi terakhir yang menjadi kode etik dalam peperangan, dan hal ini tetap tak tertandingi hingga hari ini, karena masih diajarkan kepada anak-anak sekolah di seluruh Dunia Islam. Di antara instruksi tersebut adalah: "Janganlah kalian berlaku curang maupun berkhianat. Jangan mencuri dari harta rampasan yang kalian peroleh. Jangan mencincang mayat musuh. Jangan membunuh orang tua, anak-anak, dan wanita. Jangan membakar pohon kurma. Jangan menebang pohon buah-buahan. Jangan menyembelih kambing, sapi, atau unta, kecuali untuk kebutuhan makan. Kalian akan menjumpai orang-orang yang mengasingkan diri untuk beribadah dan tinggal di biara-biara, maka biarkanlah mereka."

Ekspedisi Usamah terbukti sangat berhasil, karena ia menyerbu distrik-distrik perbatasan Syam dan kembali ke Madinah setelah empat puluh hari.

Ekspedisi ini juga membuahkan hasil baik lainnya. Ia menjadi pembuka mata bagi mereka yang mengira Islam sedang sekarat. Mereka mendapat bukti nyata bahwa Islam masih mampu menantang salah satu kekuatan terbesar di dunia. Hal ini membuat gentar suku-suku yang ragu, dan sebagian suku yang telah meninggalkan Islam benar-benar kembali memeluknya.

Kemenangan-kemenangan berturut-turut kaum Muslimin semasa hidup Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, memberikan dampak besar dalam membawa banyak suku yang tinggal jauh dari Madinah ke dalam Islam. Sebagian suku ini memeluk Islam karena mengagumi kemenangan yang diraih kaum Muslimin, sebagian memeluknya demi memperoleh keuntungan duniawi, dan sebagian menerima Islam karena keimanan yang mendalam dan keyakinan yang kuat. Ini berarti bahwa sejumlah besar kaum Muslimin belum sepenuhnya melepaskan sisa-sisa Jahiliyyah, terutama perilaku dan kebiasaan yang menjadi ciri masyarakat sebelum Islam. Sebagian kaum Muslimin masih dijangkiti rasisme dan kesombongan, yang menimbulkan kebencian dan perasaan buruk di antara suku-suku dan membawa mereka kembali ke kebiasaan pra-Islam. Dengan demikian, akidah Islam belum sepenuhnya tertanam kuat di hati sebagian kaum Muslimin. Allah Ta'ala berfirman:

"Orang-orang Arab Badui berkata, 'Kami telah beriman.' Katakanlah, 'Kamu belum beriman, tetapi katakanlah, "Kami telah tunduk," karena iman belum masuk ke dalam hatimu. Dan jika kamu taat kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya, Dia tidak akan mengurangi sedikit pun (pahala) amalmu. Sesungguhnya Allah Maha Pengampun, Maha Penyayang.'" (Al-Hujurat: 14)

Itulah sebabnya ketika ~Uyaynah Ibn Hisn, pemimpin suku Bani Fazarah, berkata saat mendukung Tulayhah yang mengaku sebagai Nabi: "Seorang Nabi dari dua sekutu (yaitu suku Asad dan Ghatafaan) jauh lebih baik bagi kami daripada seorang Nabi dari Quraysh."

Semangat rasisme Jahiliyah mencapai puncaknya pada sebagian orang yang bertindak berlebihan dan mengaku sebagai Nabi, demi meraih kebanggaan dan kedudukan di antara kaum mereka yang tidak kalah dari apa yang dinikmati Quraysh ketika Islam menang pada masa hidup Nabi, semoga rahmat dan berkah Allah tercurah kepadanya. Hal ini disebabkan oleh kesalahpahaman mereka terhadap Islam, agama yang tidak membedakan antara Quraysh dan suku-suku lainnya, bahkan jika mereka bukan bangsa Arab.

Dengan demikian, Al-Aswad Al-'Ansi mulai meyakinkan suku Mudhajaj bahwa ia adalah seorang Nabi yang menerima wahyu dari langit, dan ia didukung oleh sebagian kaumnya; Musaylamah Al-Kadzdzab (si pendusta) mengaku sebagai Nabi di suku Banu Hanifah; dan Tulayhah Al-Asadi di suku Banu Asad juga mengaku sebagai Nabi; demikian pula, Sajah mengaku sebagai Nabi di suku Banu Tamim.
content_ar: One of the Ansaar said: "lam the rubbing post and the fruitful propped-up palm*. Let us have two rulers: one from us and another from you, O people of Quraysh." Altercation waxed hotter and voices were raised حتى I was afraid lest a complete disunity should take place, لذا I said, ‘Stretch out your hand, O أبو بكر. He did لذا and I paid him homage, then the Muhajireen swore the pledge of allegiance and لذا did the Ansaar afterwards. Le. aman who can الدواء people's ills and ts held in high esteem on account of his great experience and الخير counsel, {trans.) “عمر added: “By الله, there was no grater problem than the election of أبو بكر; we were afraid that إذا we left the people, they might pledge allegiance بعد us to one of their men, in which case we would have given them our consent for something against our real wish, or would have opposed them and stirred commotion. لذا إذا anyone pledge allegiance to someone without consulting other المسلمون, then the one he has selected should not be given allegiance, lest both of them should be killed." On the authority of Ibn Mas* ood, may الله be pleased with him, who says: Upon the demise of الله's Messenger, peace and البركات be upon him, the Ansaar suggested that there should be two leaders (one from the Ansaar and another from the Muhajireen), حتى وأخيراً ~عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab intervened, saying: "O people of the Ansaar, know you not that الله's Messenger, peace and البركات be upon him, ordered أبو بكر to lead the people in Salaal? Which of you will willingly take precedence over أبو بكر?" Thereupon the Ansaar said, "الله forbid that ينبغي لنا take precedence over أبو بكر!" fast to it, الله will guide you as He guided him. الله has placed your affairs in the hands of the best of you, the Companion of الله's Messenger, and the "ثانياً of the two who were in the cave". لذا arise and pledge allegiance to him. Thereupon the people pledged allegiance to أبو بكر. بعد the pledge in the hall (of Banu Saa* idah). Then أبو بكر, may الله be pleased with him, got up, praised and thanked الله as He deserves, and then addressed the assembly وهكذا: "O people, I have been given authority over you, yet [ am not the best of you. إذا 1 do well, give me your support, and إذا I do الخطأ, set me الحق. الحق is loyalty, and lying is treachery. The weak amongst you shall be strong in my eyes, حتى I secure his الحقوق, Jnsha-الله (الله willing); and the strong amongst you shall be weak in my eyes, حتى I wrest from him the الحقوق of others, Insha-الله. إذا a people give up Jihad in the Cause of الله, الله will smite them with humiliation. And إذا obscenity permeates among a people, الله will inflict calamities upon them. Obey me as long as I obey الله and His Messenger, and إذا [I disobey الله and His Messenger, you owe me no obedience. Arise for الصلاة, الله have الرحمة upon you!" Such was the Magna Charta the أولاً Caliph of الإسلام, granted his peo
